summary: [BE] Form Package — API Insert TaskPersonalizedLog Session (with 6 Snapshot Fields)
desc:
[H2] Background | Setiap kali mechanic tap "Mulai" (Start) pada form di PM Shutdown, mobile client membuat TaskPersonalizedLog session record secara lokal dan meng-queue-nya untuk sync ke server. API ini adalah endpoint yang menerima data session tersebut dari mobile. | Prinsip penting: server hanya menerima dan menyimpan data — tidak ada logic auto-close atau deteksi shift di server. Semua logic tersebut dijalankan di client (lihat IAMS30-4322 dan IAMS30-4340). | [H2] Endpoint | POST /api/task-personalized-logs

Body (per session):
{
  "taskPersonalizedId": "guid",
  "startDate": "datetime",       // device clock saat tap Mulai
  "endDate": "datetime|null",     // null jika session masih open, atau sudah di-close client-side
  "shiftName": "string",          // e.g. "Day Shift" — snapshot saat session dibuat
  "userFullName": "string",       // snapshot dari UserEmploymentProfile
  "siteCode": "string",           // snapshot
  "siteName": "string",           // snapshot
  "sectionId": "string",          // snapshot
  "sectionName": "string|null"    // snapshot, nullable
} | Catatan: mobile mengirim sessions dalam batch sync yang sama dengan operasi lain (Assign to Me, field updates). Tidak ada sync terpisah per operasi. | [H2] Server-side behavior | - Server INSERT record ke TaskPersonalizedLog dengan semua field yang dikirim. | - Jika session dengan Id yang sama sudah ada (retry): UPDATE EndDate dan fields yang berubah (upsert by Id). | - 6 snapshot fields (ShiftName, UserFullName, SiteCode, SiteName, SectionId, SectionName) disimpan as-is dari request — server tidak lookup ke UserEmploymentProfile. | - Semua 6 snapshot fields nullable: jika tidak dikirim, simpan NULL (backward compatibility untuk mobile build lama). | - startDate dan endDate menggunakan device clock — server tidak overwrite dengan server timestamp. | [H2] Activity records setelah form Complete | TaskPersonalizedLog dari mechanic yang masih offline saat form di-submit TETAP DITERIMA saat sync, selama StartDate < submitted time. Activity records adalah historical log yang tidak mempengaruhi form content dan tidak pernah ditolak. | // Rule penerimaan:
IF record.startDate < task.completedAt:
  ACCEPT  // historical, simpan apa adanya
ELSE:
  REJECT  // startDate setelah submit time, tidak masuk akal | [H2] Relationship dengan tickets lain | - IAMS30-4321 + subtask IAMS30-4338: DDL 6 kolom baru di TaskPersonalizedLog. | - IAMS30-4364: Update request body untuk accept 6 snapshot fields. | - IAMS30-4322 + IAMS30-4340: Client-side logic yang menghasilkan data yang dikirim ke endpoint ini. | - IAMS30-4323: Auto-close via Submit — juga menggunakan endpoint ini untuk update EndDate sessions yang masih open. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - API menerima session data dan menyimpan ke TaskPersonalizedLog dengan benar. | - 6 snapshot fields tersimpan as-is dari request (tidak di-lookup server-side). | - Semua 6 fields nullable — request tanpa fields tidak error (backward compat). | - Session dengan StartDate < completedAt diterima bahkan setelah Task.Status = Complete. | - Upsert by session Id: idempotent, retry aman. | - Test: insert normal, insert after Complete (accepted), insert after Complete (rejected — startDate > completedAt), retry idempotency.

summary: [BE] Form Package — Per-Field Sync: First Write Wins, Batching, Conflict Resolution, Auto-Sync, dan Submit Form
desc:
[H2] Background | Beberapa mechanic bisa mengisi satu form bersamaan secara offline-first. Sistem harus mengelola sinkronisasi per-field (bukan per-dokumen), mendeteksi dan meresolusi konflik, serta menangani Submit Form sebagai aksi final yang mengunci form. | [H2] A — Per-Field Save & Sync | [H3] Granularitas sync: per field + batching | Client hanya mengirim field yang berubah (dirty fields). Semua dirty fields dikirim dalam satu batch request per sync session — satu network round trip. | POST /api/form-submissions/{formSubmissionId}/sync
Body: {
  requestId: uuid,         // idempotency per batch
  fields: [
    { tabId, fieldId, value, timestamp, updatedBy },
    ...
  ]
}

Response: {
  accepted: [fieldId, ...],
  rejected: [{ fieldId, reason: "already_written_by_other", writtenBy: "Mechanic A" }]
} | [H3] Conflict resolution: First Write Wins per field | Setiap field di Cosmos menyimpan: value, timestamp (device clock saat input), updatedBy. | // Server-side conflict resolution per field:
IF existing.timestamp < incoming.timestamp:
  DISCARD incoming  // existing wins
  REPORT field sebagai "rejected" ke client
ELIF existing.timestamp == incoming.timestamp:
  DISCARD incoming  // no-op, dianggap berhasil (idempotency)
ELSE:
  UPDATE field dengan incoming value  // incoming wins | Known limitation: timestamp menggunakan device clock — bisa tidak akurat. Acceptable risk karena kemungkinan dua mechanic mengisi field yang sama di momen identik sangat kecil di lapangan tambang. | [H3] Idempotency key & timestamp collision | - Idempotency key per field: fieldId + timestamp. | - Jika dua mechanic submit field yang sama dengan timestamp identik (device clock tidak sinkron): sync pertama diproses, sync berikutnya dianggap berhasil (no-op) — tidak overwrite, tidak error. | - Setiap batch diberi unique requestId. Partial failure -> retry batch yang sama -> server tidak proses field yang sudah diproses (idempotent by fieldId+timestamp). | [H3] Auto-sync triggers (aktif saat ada koneksi) | Trigger || Aksi || Interval | After input (debounce) || Upload dirty fields + download latest dari server || 3 detik setelah input terakhir | Background timer || Download latest saja (tanpa upload jika tidak ada dirty) || Setiap 30 detik | Auto-sync aktif hanya saat ada koneksi. Timer 30 detik mempengaruhi battery — trade-off yang sudah diterima. | [H3] Visibility antar mechanic | Mechanic bisa saling melihat progress pengisian rekannya secara near real-time per field (bukan per tab). Auto-sync yang aktif setiap 3-30 detik memastikan update dari mechanic lain terlihat tanpa perlu persistent connection. | [H3] Sistem tidak tracking device | Sistem tidak perlu tahu device mana saja yang sedang mengerjakan form yang sama. Koordinasi sepenuhnya tanggung jawab operasional mechanic dan supervisor. | [H2] B — Submit Form (aksi final) | Submit dilakukan SEKALI oleh SATU mechanic sebagai perwakilan. Submit mengunci form secara final — tidak bisa diubah setelah submit. Lihat subtask IAMS30-TBD untuk implementasi endpoint submit. | [H3] Rules | - Submit dilakukan sekali oleh satu mechanic — berlaku untuk semua mechanic yang mengerjakan form tersebut. | - Pre-condition: semua mandatory fields terisi di device mechanic yang akan submit (validasi client-side). | - Submit bisa offline — di-queue, dikirim ke server saat koneksi tersedia. | - Duplicate submit (dua mechanic submit bersamaan): yang pertama tiba di server yang berlaku (first submit wins by server receive time). | - Post-submit: Task.Status = Complete, form masuk approval workflow (sama seperti Form Submission existing). | [H3] Review sebelum submit | Mechanic perwakilan dapat mereview form sebelum submit melalui fitur Summary yang sudah ada di Form Submission (reuse existing). | [H2] Access Control | Siapa || Aksi yang diperbolehkan | Semua user dengan akses PM Shutdown workcard || Assign to Me, buka form (view), tap Start, isi form, sync | Mechanic yang sudah assign + tap Start || Isi dan submit form | Supervisor / Foreman || Finish Execution | Mechanic dengan permission PM_Shutdown_Finish_Execution || Finish Execution | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Sync endpoint menerima batch field updates dan memproses First Write Wins per field. | - Rejected fields dikembalikan dalam response dengan alasan. | - Batch idempotent: mengirim batch yang sama dua kali tidak menghasilkan perubahan data. | - Timestamp collision: field dengan timestamp identik dianggap no-op, tidak error. | - Auto-sync debounce 3s aktif setelah input. Timer 30s untuk background pull. | - Conflict notification UX ditangani oleh IAMS30-4371.

subtasks.summary: Mobile: show conflict notification for fields rejected by First Write Wins on sync response
BE: Submit Form endpoint — validate mandatory fields server-side, Task.Status=Complete, approval workflow
subtasks.desc: [H2] Background | Setelah mechanic tap Submit, mobile mengirim request ke server. Server harus: (1) validate semua mandatory fields terisi, (2) update Task.Status=Complete, (3) trigger approval workflow, (4) auto-close open TaskPersonalizedLog sessions (ditangani oleh IAMS30-4343). | [H2] Endpoint | POST /api/form-submissions/{formSubmissionId}/submit | [H2] Processing | - [1] Validate: Task.Status bukan Complete atau Approved (tolak jika sudah final). | - [2] Validate mandatory fields server-side: semua field dengan isMandatory=true di Cosmos harus terisi. Jika ada yang kosong: return 400 dengan list field yang belum terisi. | - [3] UPDATE Task.Status = Complete, Task.CompletedAt = server timestamp. | - [4] Trigger approval workflow (sama seperti Form Submission existing). | - [5] Auto-close open TaskPersonalizedLog sessions: ditangani oleh handler IAMS30-4343 (min(shiftEnd, submitTime)). | [H2] Duplicate submit handling | - Jika Task.Status sudah Complete saat request diterima: return 200 (idempotent, bukan error). | - First submit wins — jika dua mechanic submit bersamaan, yang pertama tiba di server yang mengubah status. Yang kedua mendapat 200 (sudah Complete). | [H2] Offline behavior | - Submit bisa dilakukan offline. Mobile queue-kan request. | - Saat sync: server melakukan final validation. Jika mandatory fields tidak lengkap di server (mechanic lain belum sync), return 400 — mobile menampilkan error. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Submit berhasil: Task.Status = Complete, approval workflow triggered. | - Submit pada Task yang sudah Complete: return 200 idempotent. | - Submit dengan mandatory fields tidak lengkap: return 400 dengan detail fields yang kurang. | - Test: normal submit, duplicate submit, mandatory incomplete, offline-then-sync.

summary: [BE] Form Package — Update Task.Status to In Progress on TaskPersonalized Sync Using Minimum StartedAt
desc: [H2] Background | When a mechanic's  | TaskPersonalized |  record is synced to the server, the corresponding  | Task.Status |  must be updated to  | In Progress | . In a multi-mechanic scenario where multiple  | TaskPersonalized |  records are synced at different times (due to offline gaps),  | Task.Status |  must use the smallest  | StartedAt |  across all records — not the order in which they arrive at the server. | [H2] Objective | Implement server-side logic to update  | Task.Status = In Progress |  upon receiving  | TaskPersonalized |  sync, using the minimum  | StartedAt |  across all  | TaskPersonalized |  records for that  | Task | . | [H2] Scope | - Service:  | maintenance-execution | - Trigger:  | TaskPersonalized |  upsert (sync from mobile) | - Table affected:  | Task | - Depends on: IAMS30-4273 (Tab Form — Assign to Me), IAMS30-4274 (Tap card render form) | [H2] Requirements | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Trigger || When a  | TaskPersonalized |  record is upserted (created or updated) via sync | 2 || Target || The  | Task |  record linked to the upserted  | TaskPersonalized.TaskId | 3 || Condition || Only update  | Task.Status → In Progress |  if current  | Task.Status = Open | 4 || InProgressAt |  value || Set  | Task.InProgressAt = MIN(StartedAt) |  across all  | TaskPersonalized |  records for this  | TaskId | 5 || Multi-mechanic || If multiple  | TaskPersonalized |  records exist (multi-mechanic), always use the smallest  | StartedAt |  — regardless of which record was synced first | 6 || Idempotent || If  | Task.Status |  is already  | In Progress | ,  | Complete | , or  | Approved |  — do not downgrade or overwrite | [H2] Business Rules | - Task.InProgressAt |  reflects the earliest moment any mechanic started the form — not the server receive time. | - If Mechanic A syncs at 10:00 with  | StartedAt = 08:00 | , and Mechanic B syncs at 11:00 with  | StartedAt = 07:30 |  →  | Task.InProgressAt |  must be updated to  | 07:30 |  on B's sync. | - This logic handles offline gaps: a mechanic who started earlier but synced later must still be reflected correctly. | - Task.Status |  is never downgraded —  | Complete |  or  | Approved |  tasks are not affected by new  | TaskPersonalized |  syncs. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Task.Status |  changes from  | Open |  to  | In Progress |  on first  | TaskPersonalized |  sync for that Task. | - Task.InProgressAt |  is set to  | MIN(StartedAt) |  across all  | TaskPersonalized |  records for the Task. | - If a later-synced  | TaskPersonalized |  has an earlier  | StartedAt | ,  | Task.InProgressAt |  is updated to the earlier value. | - Task.Status = Complete |  or  | Approved |  is never overwritten by this logic. | - Logic is idempotent — re-syncing the same  | TaskPersonalized |  produces no unintended side effects. | - No regression on existing  | TaskPersonalized |  upsert behavior.

subtasks.summary: Extend TaskPersonalized upsert handler: set Task.Status → In Progress + Task.InProgressAt = MIN(StartedAt)
subtasks.desc: Scope: | Services.iAMS.MaintenanceExec |  —  | TaskPersonalized |  upsert handler (called when mobile syncs mechanic task progress). | Logic to add: | - After upserting the  | TaskPersonalized |  record, check if the parent  | Task |  is still in a pre-progress status (not  | Complete | , not  | Approved |  — never downgrade those). | - Query  | MIN(TaskPersonalized.StartedAt) |  across all  | TaskPersonalized |  records for this  | TaskId |  where  | StartedAt IS NOT NULL | . | - If a  | MIN |  value is found and  | Task.Status |  is not already  | In Progress |  /  | Complete |  /  | Approved | : set  | Task.Status = In Progress |  and  | Task.InProgressAt = MIN(StartedAt) | . | - Run inside the existing upsert transaction — no separate endpoint. | ⚠️ DB column dependency (not yet created): | - TaskPersonalized.StartedAt |  (DateTime, nullable) — product team will create the DB ticket. | - Task.InProgressAt |  (DateTime, nullable) — product team will create the DB ticket. | Both columns must exist before this handler logic is deployed. Implement the handler logic now; guard with a null-check on  | StartedAt |  so existing syncs without  | StartedAt |  are unaffected. | Acceptance criteria: | - First mechanic sync with a  | StartedAt |  value transitions  | Task.Status → In Progress | . | - Task.InProgressAt |  equals the earliest  | StartedAt |  across all mechanics on this task. | - Re-syncing (idempotent): calling upsert again with the same data does not change status back. | - Task already  | Complete |  or  | Approved | : handler skips the status update entirely.

subtasks.summary: Tests: In Progress transition, MIN across multi-mechanic, idempotent, no downgrade of Complete/Approved
subtasks.desc: Scope: |  xUnit tests for the Task.Status → In Progress logic added to the  | TaskPersonalized |  upsert handler in  | Services.iAMS.MaintenanceExec | . | Test cases: | - First sync with StartedAt: |  Task status is  | Pending | ; one mechanic syncs with  | StartedAt |  set →  | Task.Status = In Progress | ,  | Task.InProgressAt = StartedAt | . | - MIN across multiple mechanics: |  Two mechanics sync; Mechanic B has earlier  | StartedAt |  than Mechanic A →  | Task.InProgressAt = Mechanic B's StartedAt | . | - Idempotent re-sync: |  Same  | TaskPersonalized |  synced twice with identical  | StartedAt |  →  | Task.InProgressAt |  unchanged, status remains  | In Progress |  (no duplicate update). | - No downgrade — Complete: |  Task already  | Complete | ; mechanic syncs → status stays  | Complete | ,  | InProgressAt |  not changed. | - No downgrade — Approved: |  Task already  | Approved | ; mechanic syncs → status stays  | Approved | . | - No StartedAt (legacy sync): | TaskPersonalized.StartedAt |  is null → no  | Task.Status |  change, no error. | Use Moq for repository interfaces. Stub  | MIN(StartedAt) |  query return values to exercise all paths without a real DB.

[MachineSMUAddress]  NVARCHAR(100) NULL, | [H2] TaskPersonalized.sql — Add StartedAt, Remove SMU cols | [StartedAt] DATETIME NULL, | Remove MachineSMUValue and MachineSMUAddress lines from CREATE TABLE definition (SSDT — never write DROP COLUMN manually). | [H2] Deployment Order | - 1. subtasks.summary: Migration script: copy MachineSMUValue/Address from TaskPersonalized to Task before column drop
subtasks.desc:
[H2] Purpose | Copy existing SMU values from TaskPersonalized to the corresponding Task record before removing the columns from TaskPersonalized. Must run between the two SSDT deployments. | [H2] Script | -- Run AFTER Task.sql deployed, BEFORE TaskPersonalized.sql updated
UPDATE t
SET t.MachineSMUValue = sub.MachineSMUValue,
    t.MachineSMUAddress = sub.MachineSMUAddress
FROM dbo.Task t
INNER JOIN (
  SELECT TaskId,
         MAX(MachineSMUValue) AS MachineSMUValue,
         MAX(MachineSMUAddress) AS MachineSMUAddress
  FROM dbo.TaskPersonalized
  WHERE MachineSMUValue IS NOT NULL OR MachineSMUAddress IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY TaskId
) sub ON sub.TaskId = t.Id
WHERE t.MachineSMUValue IS NULL;

-- Verify (should return 0):
SELECT COUNT(*) AS should_be_zero
FROM dbo.Task t
INNER JOIN dbo.TaskPersonalized tp ON tp.TaskId = t.Id
WHERE (tp.MachineSMUValue IS NOT NULL OR tp.MachineSMUAddress IS NOT NULL)
  AND t.MachineSMUValue IS NULL; | [H2] Execution Steps | - 1. Run in dev — verify COUNT = 0 from verification query. | - 2. Run in staging — verify. | - 3. Include in production deployment runbook between the two SSDT deploys. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - All Task records that had corresponding TaskPersonalized SMU data are populated after migration. | - Verification query returns 0. | - Script is idempotent (safe to run twice without side effects).

subtasks.summary: Update WorkOrder.Status to In Progress on first TaskPersonalized sync for that WorkOrder
subtasks.desc:
[H2] Background | When the first mechanic assigns themselves to a form and syncs their TaskPersonalized record, the parent WorkOrder status must transition from Open to In Progress. This signals that execution has started. The transition is triggered by the first TaskPersonalized sync for any Task belonging to that WorkOrder — not by a separate explicit action. | [H2] Logic | -- On TaskPersonalized sync (upsert):
IF WorkOrder.Status = "Open"
  AND at least 1 TaskPersonalized record now exists for any Task in this WorkOrder:
  UPDATE WorkOrder SET Status = "In Progress"

-- Status is idempotent: if already In Progress or Complete, do not downgrade. | [H2] Scope | - Service: Services.iAMS.MaintenanceExec | - Triggered inside the TaskPersonalized upsert handler (same handler extended in IAMS30-4324 base scope). | - WorkOrder.Status transition: Open -> In Progress only. Never downgrade from In Progress or Complete. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - WorkOrder.Status changes to In Progress on the first TaskPersonalized sync for that WorkOrder. | - Second or subsequent TaskPersonalized syncs do not change WorkOrder.Status if already In Progress. | - WorkOrder.Status is never downgraded from In Progress or Complete by this logic. | - Test: Open WorkOrder -> first sync -> In Progress; second sync -> still In Progress; Complete WorkOrder -> sync -> still Complete.

summary: [BE] Form Package — Auto-close Open TaskPersonalizedLog Sessions on Form Submit
desc: [H2] Background | When a mechanic submits a form in PM Shutdown, the submission acts as a safety net to auto-close any open  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  sessions for all mechanics working on that form. This handles cases where mechanics forget to click "Finish/Close" before the form is submitted. | [H2] Objective | On form submission, auto-close all open  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  records for the submitted Task — setting  | EndDate = min(shift end time, submit time) |  for each open session. | [H2] Scope | - Service:  | maintenance-execution | - Trigger: Form submission endpoint | - Table affected:  | TaskPersonalizedLog | - Depends on: IAMS30-4321 (ShiftName column), IAMS30-4280 (SiteShift table) | [H2] Requirements | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Trigger || On successful form submission (Task.Status → Complete), find all open  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  records for the submitted  | TaskId | 2 || Target records || All  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  where  | TaskPersonalizedId |  belongs to the submitted  | TaskId |  AND  | EndDate IS NULL | 3 || EndDate calculation || For each open session: resolve shift end time from  | SiteShift |  using  | TenantCode + SiteCode + session.StartDate | . Then:  | EndDate = min(shift end time, submit time) | 4 || Night shift handling || If  | SiteShift.EndShift < SiteShift.StartShift |  (overnight): shift end date = session StartDate + 1 day | 5 || Fallback || If no matching  | SiteShift |  found → set  | EndDate = submit time | 6 || Scope || Only sessions for the submitted  | TaskId |  are auto-closed — not sessions for other tasks in the same WorkOrder | [H2] Business Rules | - Submit is a  | safety net |  — it handles the last open session that was not closed via the "Mulai at new shift" trigger. | - EndDate = min(shift end time, submit time) |  ensures session duration never exceeds the shift it started in. | - Example: session started at 08:00 Day Shift (end = 18:00), form submitted at 11:00 →  | EndDate = min(18:00, 11:00) = 11:00 | . | - Example: session started at 08:00 Day Shift, form submitted at 19:00 (Night Shift) →  | EndDate = min(18:00, 19:00) = 18:00 | . | - Already-closed sessions ( | EndDate IS NOT NULL | ) are never overwritten. | - TaskPersonalizedLog |  records are accepted even if mechanic was offline at submit time — as long as  | StartDate < submit time | . | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - On form submission, all open  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  for the submitted  | TaskId |  are closed. | - EndDate |  is set to  | min(shift end time, submit time) |  for each open session. | - Night shift cross-midnight calculation is handled correctly. | - Already-closed sessions are not modified. | - Fallback to  | submit time |  when no  | SiteShift |  data available. | - Auto-close does not affect  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  records for other tasks in the same WorkOrder. | - No regression on existing form submission flow. | [H2] Penting: Activity Records Setelah Form Complete | TaskPersonalizedLog records dari mechanic yang masih offline saat form di-submit TETAP DITERIMA saat sync, bahkan setelah Task.Status = Complete. Ini berbeda dari form content (field values) yang ditolak setelah Complete. | Jenis Data || Jika sync setelah form Complete || Alasan | Form content (field values) || DITOLAK — form sudah final, tidak bisa diubah || Form final tidak boleh dimodifikasi | Activity records (TaskPersonalizedLog) || DITERIMA selama StartDate < submitTime || Historical log, tidak mempengaruhi form content | Rule penerimaan activity record setelah Complete: StartDate < Task.CompletedAt. Record dengan StartDate >= CompletedAt tidak masuk akal secara kronologis dan ditolak. | [H2] Implikasi untuk auto-close handler | Auto-close handler ini (EndDate = min(shiftEnd, submitTime)) hanya berjalan pada saat submit — bukan saat sync activity records pasca-Complete. Server harus tetap menerima UPDATE EndDate pada session yang sudah ada, bahkan jika Task sudah Complete, selama data tersebut adalah penutupan sesi yang valid secara kronologis. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria (tambahan) | - TaskPersonalizedLog INSERT dengan StartDate < Task.CompletedAt diterima meski Task sudah Complete. | - TaskPersonalizedLog INSERT dengan StartDate >= Task.CompletedAt ditolak dengan 400. | - UPDATE EndDate pada session yang ada diterima meski Task sudah Complete (untuk mechanic yang sync terlambat). | - Test: late sync activity record (accepted), activity record after Complete with valid StartDate (accepted), invalid StartDate (rejected).

subtasks.summary: Extend form submit handler: auto-close open TaskPersonalizedLog with EndDate = min(ShiftEndTime, submitTime)

summary: [MOBILE] Form Package — Assign to Me, Slide to Start (Mulai), dan Finish: TaskPersonalized & TaskPersonalizedLog Management
desc:
[H2] Background | Ticket ini mencakup dua aksi berurutan yang dilakukan mechanic sebelum bisa mengisi form di PM Shutdown: | - 1. Assign to Me — mechanic mendaftarkan diri ke form (membuat TaskPersonalized). Fitur ini sudah ada di Digiman+ untuk task execution; perlu diverifikasi apakah bisa di-reuse atau perlu di-enhance untuk context PM Shutdown Form Package. | - 2. Slide to Start (Mulai) — setelah assign, mechanic membuka form dan slide untuk mulai mengisi. Aksi ini membuat TaskPersonalizedLog session dan menangani auto-close sesi sebelumnya jika shift berganti. | - 3. Finish — mechanic menutup form sementara tanpa submit. Menutup TaskPersonalizedLog session dengan EndDate = actual tap time. | Ketiga aksi ini offline-first dan saling berkaitan erat dalam satu flow, sehingga dikelola dalam satu ticket. | [H2] Bagian A — Assign to Me (TaskPersonalized) | [H3] Langkah 1: Investigasi existing API (WAJIB sebelum implementasi) | Fitur "Assign to Me" / "Assign to" sudah ada di Digiman+. Developer harus investigasi terlebih dahulu: | - Temukan endpoint existing yang menangani pembuatan / upsert TaskPersonalized. | - Apakah endpoint sudah upsert by TaskId + UserCode (idempotent)? | - Apakah bisa dipanggil dari Tab Form mobile untuk context PM Shutdown Form Package tanpa perubahan? | - Apakah ada validasi tambahan yang perlu ditambahkan (Task.Status = Complete/Approved harus ditolak)? | [H3] Langkah 2: Pilih pendekatan berdasarkan investigasi | Kondisi || Pendekatan | Existing API sudah handle semua requirement, tidak ada risiko breaking || REUSE — pastikan mobile memanggil endpoint yang benar. Dokumentasikan di PR. | Existing API mendekati requirement tapi perlu perubahan kecil backward-compatible || ENHANCE — modifikasi endpoint existing. Pastikan test existing tetap hijau. | Existing API terlalu berbeda atau perubahan akan breaking existing callers || NEW API — buat endpoint baru (misal POST /api/tasks/{taskId}/assign-to-me) yang idempotent. | [H3] Requirement fungsional Assign to Me (apapun pendekatannya) | - Upsert by TaskId + UserCode — idempotent. Assign berkali-kali dari device berbeda tidak menghasilkan duplikasi TaskPersonalized. | - UserCode dari authenticated JWT — mechanic tidak bisa assign ke user lain. | - Task.Status = Complete atau Approved → tolak dengan 400. | - TenantCode isolation dari JWT. | - Offline: TaskPersonalized dibuat lokal saat offline, di-sync ke server via upsert saat koneksi tersedia. | [H2] Bagian B — Slide to Start / Mulai (TaskPersonalizedLog) | [H3] Client-side logic saat tap Mulai | function handleStartTap(taskPersonalizedId, deviceTimestamp, siteCode):
  currentShift = getShift(deviceTimestamp, siteCode)  // from local SiteShift cache

  // 1. Cek: ada open session di shift yang sama?
  openSameShift = TaskPersonalizedLog.find(
    taskPersonalizedId, ShiftName=currentShift.Name, EndDate=null
  )
  if openSameShift: return  // skip, langsung masuk form

  // 2. Auto-close session dari shift lain (jika ada)
  openOther = TaskPersonalizedLog.find(taskPersonalizedId, EndDate=null)
  if openOther:
    prevShift = getShift(openOther.StartDate, siteCode)
    openOther.EndDate = calculateShiftEnd(openOther.StartDate, prevShift)
    queue UPDATE openOther

  // 3. Buat session baru
  queue INSERT TaskPersonalizedLog(
    taskPersonalizedId,
    StartDate=deviceTimestamp,
    ShiftName=currentShift.Name,
    UserFullName=currentUser.fullName,   // snapshot
    SiteCode=currentUser.siteCode,       // snapshot
    SiteName=currentUser.siteName,       // snapshot
    SectionId=currentUser.sectionId,     // snapshot
    SectionName=currentUser.sectionName  // snapshot
  )
  // Semua operasi dikirim dalam satu batch saat sync | Catatan: 6 snapshot columns (ShiftName, UserFullName, SiteCode, SiteName, SectionId, SectionName) harus dikirim ke server saat sync — sesuai scope IAMS30-4321 dan IAMS30-4364. | [H2] Bagian C — Finish (close session sementara) | function handleFinishTap(taskPersonalizedId, deviceTimestamp):
  openSession = TaskPersonalizedLog.find(taskPersonalizedId, EndDate=null)
  if not openSession: return  // no-op

  openSession.EndDate = deviceTimestamp  // actual tap time, bukan shift end
  queue UPDATE openSession | Berbeda dari auto-close (shift end time): explicit Finish tap menggunakan waktu aktual tap. PRD Skenario 10: mechanic tap Finish pukul 19:00 meski shift berakhir 18:00 → EndDate = 19:00. | [H2] Business Rules (gabungan) | - Assign to Me harus dilakukan sebelum Mulai bisa diakses — form card hanya bisa dibuka (view) sebelum assign. | - Mulai muncul setiap kali mechanic yang sudah assign membuka form (bukan hanya pertama kali). | - Deduplication TaskPersonalizedLog: max 1 open session per mechanic per shift (by TaskPersonalizedId + ShiftName where EndDate IS NULL). | - Finish hanya menutup session tanpa submit form — mechanic bisa Mulai lagi di shift yang sama atau berikutnya. | - Semua operasi fully offline — di-queue dalam satu batch sync. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Assign to Me: hasil investigasi existing API terdokumentasi di PR. Pendekatan dipilih (reuse/enhance/new) dengan alasan. | - Assign to Me idempotent: memanggil dua kali tidak duplikasi TaskPersonalized. | - Assign ke Task yang sudah Complete/Approved dikembalikan 400. | - Slide to Start: membuat TaskPersonalizedLog session baru dengan 6 snapshot fields terisi. | - Slide to Start di shift yang sama: no-op (tidak buat session baru). | - Slide to Start di shift berbeda: auto-close session lama (EndDate = shift end time), buat session baru. | - Finish tap: set EndDate = actual timestamp pada open session. No-op jika tidak ada open session. | - Semua aksi bekerja offline — di-queue dan sync ke server saat koneksi tersedia. | - Tidak ada regression pada existing Assign to Me / task assignment flow yang sudah berjalan.

subtasks.summary: Mulai handler: shift resolution, session deduplication, auto-close on shift change
subtasks.desc:
Scope: |  Flutter mobile —  | module_maintenance_strategy |  or  | module_inspection |  (whichever owns PM Shutdown execution), Mulai button tap handler. | Logic to implement: | - Resolve current shift from locally cached SiteShift (TenantCode + SiteCode + current device time → match StartShift/EndShift; overnight shifts: EndShift < StartShift means EndDate = start date + 1 day). | - Check if an open  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  already exists for this TaskPersonalizedId: |    -  | Same shift (ShiftName matches): |  skip — return early, no new record created. |    -  | Different shift: |  auto-close the open log ( | EndDate = min(previous ShiftEndTime, now) | ), then create a new  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  with the new ShiftName. |    -  | No open log: |  create a new  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  (ShiftName from resolved shift, or  | null |  if SiteShift cache is empty/unavailable — graceful fallback). | - All writes go to Isar first; enqueue to batch sync queue for server push. | - TaskPersonalized.StartedAt |  must be set on first Mulai (dependency: server-side DB column from ticket assigned by product team — implement field in local Isar schema regardless). | Acceptance criteria: | - Tapping Mulai twice in the same shift creates exactly one log entry. | - Tapping Mulai after midnight (new shift) closes the previous log and opens a new one. | - Works fully offline; sync queued correctly. | - Falls back gracefully (ShiftName = null) if SiteShift cache is empty. | [H2] 6 Snapshot Fields — Wajib Disertakan dalam Sync Payload | Saat client mengirim TaskPersonalizedLog session ke server (via IAMS30-4359), payload HARUS menyertakan 6 snapshot fields yang diambil dari data lokal user pada saat session dibuat (bukan real-time dari server): | Field || Sumber data di mobile || Nullable | shiftName || Hasil getShift() dari SiteShift local cache || No — selalu ada (dari cache atau default) | userFullName || Local user profile (dari login session) || No | siteCode || Local user profile || No | siteName || Local user profile || Yes | sectionId || Local user profile || Yes | sectionName || Local user profile || Yes — bisa NULL jika tidak ada di profil | Snapshot diambil SAAT SESSION DIBUAT (saat tap Mulai), bukan saat sync. Tujuannya: jika mechanic pindah site/section setelah session dibuat, data historis tetap mencerminkan posisi saat aktivitas terjadi. | [H3] Integrasi dengan handleStartTap | function handleStartTap(taskPersonalizedId, deviceTimestamp, siteCode):
  currentShift = getShift(deviceTimestamp, siteCode)  // dari SiteShift cache
  currentUser = getLocalUserProfile()                   // dari login session

  // ... dedup + auto-close logic ...

  // Buat session baru dengan snapshot:
  queue INSERT TaskPersonalizedLog(
    taskPersonalizedId = taskPersonalizedId,
    startDate     = deviceTimestamp,
    endDate       = null,
    shiftName     = currentShift.Name,
    userFullName  = currentUser.fullName,
    siteCode      = currentUser.siteCode,
    siteName      = currentUser.siteName,
    sectionId     = currentUser.sectionId,
    sectionName   = currentUser.sectionName   // nullable
  ) | [H2] Acceptance Criteria (tambahan) | - handleStartTap mengambil 6 snapshot fields dari local user profile dan SiteShift cache pada saat session dibuat. | - Semua 6 fields disertakan dalam payload saat sync ke server. | - Snapshot tidak diupdate saat session di-close (auto-close atau Finish tap) — EndDate saja yang berubah. | - Unit test: session payload mengandung 6 fields dengan nilai dari local profile; sectionName nullable ditest dengan nilai NULL.

No network calls in unit tests.

subtasks.summary: Finish tap handler: close TaskPersonalizedLog session with actual EndDate + queue sync
subtasks.desc:
[H2] Background | After tapping "Mulai" (Start) and filling a form, a mechanic can exit the form temporarily without submitting — this is the "Finish" action. Tapping Finish must close the current open TaskPersonalizedLog session with EndDate = actual tap time. This differs from auto-close (which uses shift end time): explicit Finish means the mechanic knows exactly when they stopped. | [H2] UX | A "Finish" button/action is shown inside the form detail screen (alongside or below the form content), accessible to an assigned mechanic who has already tapped Mulai. It is hidden when the form is already Complete (submitted). | [H2] Client-side logic | function handleFinishTap(taskPersonalizedId, deviceTimestamp):
  openSession = TaskPersonalizedLog.find(
    taskPersonalizedId, EndDate=null
  )
  if not openSession: return  // no open session, no-op

  openSession.EndDate = deviceTimestamp  // actual tap time
  queue UPDATE openSession
  // queued in batch sync — not sent immediately | [H2] Key distinction from auto-close | - Auto-close (via Start in new shift) -> EndDate = shift end time of the previous session. | - Explicit Finish tap -> EndDate = actual device timestamp of the tap. | - PRD Scenario 10: mechanic taps Finish at 19:00 even though shift ended at 18:00 -> EndDate = 19:00 (actual), not 18:00. | [H2] Offline behavior | - Fully offline. EndDate set locally with device clock. | - Queued in same batch sync as other operations. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Tapping Finish sets EndDate = device timestamp on the open TaskPersonalizedLog session. | - If no open session exists, Finish tap is a no-op. | - EndDate from explicit Finish tap is the actual tap time — not shift end time. | - Form remains openable after Finish (mechanic can tap Mulai again to create a new session in the same or next shift). | - Update is queued for batch sync and works fully offline. | - Widget test: Finish sets EndDate correctly; Finish with no open session is no-op.

summary: [BE] Form Package — Modify TaskPersonalizedLog: Add 6 Snapshot Columns (ShiftName, UserFullName, SiteCode, SiteName, SectionId, SectionName)
desc: [H2] Background | The mechanic activity tracking feature for PM Shutdown Form Package requires recording contextual snapshot data at the time each session starts. This data is needed to support auto-close logic (client-side), activity reporting, and ensuring historical accuracy even after mechanics resign or transfer sites. The existing TaskPersonalizedLog table needs 6 new snapshot columns. | [H2] Objective | Add 6 snapshot columns to TaskPersonalizedLog in maintenance-execution DB. These columns are written once at session creation and are never updated, ensuring a permanent historical record of who worked where and on which shift. | [H2] Scope | - Database: cst-iams-sqldb-services-maintenance-execution | - Table: dbo.TaskPersonalizedLog | - Service: Services.iAMS.MaintenanceExec | [H2] New Columns | No || Column || Type || Nullable || Default || Description | 1 || ShiftName || NVARCHAR(100) || Yes || NULL || Name of the shift at StartDate time. e.g. "Day Shift". Resolved from SiteShift by TenantCode+SiteCode+timestamp. | 2 || UserFullName || NVARCHAR(255) || Yes || NULL || Snapshot of mechanic full name at session creation time. Source: UserEmploymentProfile.FullName. | 3 || SiteCode || NVARCHAR(50) || Yes || NULL || Snapshot of mechanic site code at session creation. Source: UserEmploymentProfile.SiteId. | 4 || SiteName || NVARCHAR(255) || Yes || NULL || Snapshot of site display name at session creation. Source: resolved from SiteCode. | 5 || SectionId || NVARCHAR(50) || Yes || NULL || Snapshot of mechanic section ID at session creation. Source: UserEmploymentProfile.SectionId. | 6 || SectionName || NVARCHAR(255) || Yes || NULL || Snapshot of section display name. Source: UserEmploymentProfile.SectionName. May be NULL if not set. | [H2] Business Rules | - All 6 columns are written once at session creation — they are immutable after creation (never updated on auto-close or submit). | - ShiftName is derived from SiteShift table by matching TenantCode + SiteCode + StartDate timestamp to the active shift. | - UserFullName, SiteCode, SiteName, SectionId, SectionName are snapshot values taken from UserEmploymentProfile at the moment the session is created — not fetched real-time on read. | - Existing records are left as NULL for all 6 columns — no backfill required. | - If SectionName is NULL in UserEmploymentProfile, store NULL — do not default to empty string. | [H2] Why Snapshot (not real-time join)? | - Mechanic transfers site/section after session created → historical data must reflect position at time of work, not current position. | - Mechanic resigns → UserEmploymentProfile record may be deleted; activity log must remain complete and queryable without JOIN to user tables. | - Reporting efficiency → avoids cross-service joins at query time. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - All 6 columns exist in TaskPersonalizedLog with correct types and nullable=YES. | - Existing records remain intact with all new columns = NULL. | - All 6 columns are populated correctly on new session creation — verified in integration test. | - None of the 6 columns are updated when EndDate is set (auto-close or explicit Finish tap). | - Migration applied without data loss in dev, staging, and production. | - No regression on existing TaskPersonalizedLog read/write flows.

summary: [BE] SiteShift — Manual Re-sync from Tenant DB (Admin Trigger)
desc: [H2] Background | Shift data in the Tenant DB may change over time (e.g., shift times adjusted, new shifts added, shifts deactivated). When this happens, the  | SiteShift |  table in  | maintenance-execution |  must be updated to reflect the latest data. A manual re-sync endpoint is provided for admin use to trigger this update on demand. | [H2] Objective | Implement a manual re-sync mechanism that allows an admin to trigger a refresh of  | SiteShift |  data in  | maintenance-execution |  from the Tenant DB, scoped by  | TenantCode |  and optionally by  | SiteCode | . | [H2] Scope | - Service:  | maintenance-execution | - Trigger: Admin action (manual) | - Source: Tenant DB | - Target:  | dbo.SiteShift |  in  | maintenance-execution | - Depends on: IAMS30-4280 (SiteShift table) | [H2] Requirements | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Scope || Re-sync by  | TenantCode |  (required) +  | SiteCode |  (optional — if omitted, re-sync all sites for that tenant) | 2 || Upsert behavior || Match existing records by  | TenantCode + SiteCode + Name | . Update if changed, insert if new. | 3 || Soft delete || Records in Tenant DB that are no longer active → set  | IsDeleted = 1 |  in execution DB | 4 || No hard delete || Never hard-delete records from  | SiteShift | 5 || Access control || Endpoint accessible by admin role only (permission:  | IAMS_Admin_SiteShift_Sync | ) | 6 || Response || Return summary: records inserted, records updated, records soft-deleted | [H2] Business Rules | - Re-sync is non-destructive — existing data is updated or soft-deleted, never hard-deleted. | - If Tenant DB is unreachable at time of re-sync, return error and do not modify  | SiteShift |  records. | - TenantCode must match the admin's own tenant — cross-tenant re-sync is not permitted. | - Re-sync does not affect  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  records already closed by the scheduler. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Admin can trigger re-sync scoped to a specific  | TenantCode |  or  | TenantCode + SiteCode | . | - New shifts from Tenant DB are inserted into  | SiteShift | . | - Changed shifts (name, start/end time) are updated in  | SiteShift | . | - Deactivated shifts in Tenant DB are soft-deleted ( | IsDeleted = 1 | ) in  | SiteShift | . | - No hard deletes occur during re-sync. | - Response includes count of records inserted, updated, and soft-deleted. | - Endpoint returns error (not partial update) if Tenant DB is unreachable. | - Cross-tenant re-sync is blocked. | - Access restricted to admin role only ( | IAMS_Admin_SiteShift_Sync | ).

summary: [BE] SiteShift — Extend Offline Sync: Include SiteShift Data for Mobile Client
desc: [H2] Background | The mechanic activity tracking feature requires shift data on the mobile device to support client-side auto-close of  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  sessions. When a mechanic taps "Mulai" in a new shift, the mobile client calculates the  | EndDate |  of the previous session using  | SiteShift.EndShift |  from local cache — no server-side scheduler involved. This means the mobile device must receive  | SiteShift |  data as part of its offline sync payload. | [H2] Objective | Extend the offline sync endpoint to include  | SiteShift |  data in the response, so the mobile device can cache shift schedules locally and use them for client-side session auto-close logic. | [H2] Scope | - Service:  | maintenance-execution | - Modified endpoint:  | GET /api/work-card/detail-offline-mode |  (or appropriate sync endpoint) | - Data source:  | dbo.SiteShift |  in  | maintenance-execution | - Depends on: IAMS30-4280 (SiteShift table + initial seed) | [H2] Requirements | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Include  | siteShifts |  in sync response || Return all active shifts for the mechanic's site ( | TenantCode + SiteCode | ) | 2 || Response fields per shift || name | ,  | startShift |  (HH:mm),  | endShift |  (HH:mm) | 3 || Filter || Only return  | IsActive = 1 |  and  | IsDeleted = 0 |  records | 4 || Scoping || Scoped by  | TenantCode + SiteCode |  from JWT — no cross-tenant/cross-site data | 5 || Delta sync || SiteShift |  data is relatively static — include in full sync (when  | lastSync |  is null). summary: [BE] SiteShift — Create Table and Initial Seed from Tenant DB
desc: [H2] Background | The auto-close mechanism for  | TaskPersonalizedLog |  requires shift data to determine when a mechanic's active session should be closed. To avoid cross-service dependency during scheduler execution, SiteShift data is maintained locally in the  | maintenance-execution |  DB, seeded from the Tenant DB. | [H2] Objective | Create the  | SiteShift |  table in  | maintenance-execution |  DB and implement initial seed logic to populate it from Tenant DB, scoped by  | TenantCode |  and  | SiteCode | . | [H2] Scope | - Database:  | cst-iams-sqldb-services-maintenance-execution | - Source: Tenant DB (existing shift data) | - New table:  | dbo.SiteShift | [H2] Requirements | Table schema: | No || Column || Type || Nullable || Default || Description | 1 || Id || BigInt || No || Auto-increment || Primary key | 2 || TenantCode || Nvarchar(50) || No || — || Tenant scoping | 3 || SiteCode || Nvarchar(50) || No || — || Site scoping | 4 || Name || Nvarchar(100) || No || — || Shift name, e.g. "Day Shift", "Night Shift" | 5 || StartShift || Time || No || — || Shift start time (local site time) | 6 || EndShift || Time || No || — || Shift end time (local site time) | 7 || IsActive || Bit || No || 1 || Whether shift is active | 8 || IsDeleted || Bit || No || 0 || Soft delete flag | 9 || CreatedBy || Nvarchar(100) || No || — ||  | 10 || CreatedAtUtc || DateTime || No || — ||  | 11 || ModifiedBy || Nvarchar(100) || Yes || NULL ||  | 12 || ModifiedAtUtc || DateTime || Yes || NULL ||  | Seed logic: | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Source || Fetch shift data from Tenant DB for all active tenants | 2 || Scoping || Seed per  | TenantCode + SiteCode |  combination | 3 || Deduplication || Upsert by  | TenantCode + SiteCode + Name |  — no duplicate shifts | 4 || Initial seed || Run once as part of migration/deployment | 5 || Soft delete || Records removed in Tenant DB must be marked  | IsDeleted = 1 |  in execution DB (not hard deleted) | [H2] Business Rules | - StartShift |  and  | EndShift |  stored in local site time — not UTC. Site timezone resolved from Tenant DB site configuration. | - Composite unique constraint on  | TenantCode + SiteCode + Name | . | - Only  | IsActive = 1 |  and  | IsDeleted = 0 |  records are used by the scheduler. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - SiteShift |  table created in  | maintenance-execution |  with schema above. | - Composite unique constraint on  | TenantCode + SiteCode + Name |  enforced. | - Initial seed populates records from Tenant DB correctly. | - Soft delete behavior: records removed in Tenant DB are marked  | IsDeleted = 1 | , not hard deleted. | - Existing data in  | maintenance-execution |  unaffected by migration.

summary: [Mobile] Separate Activity Type LOV Between Additional Mechanic Order and Additional Inspection
desc: [H3] Background | Saat ini field  | Activity Type |  pada fitur  | Additional Mechanic Order |  mengambil data dari master  | Maintenance Category |  yang terdapat pada Maintenance Strategy. | Master yang sama juga digunakan pada fitur  | Additional Inspection | , sehingga seluruh Activity Type yang tersedia akan muncul pada kedua fitur tersebut. | [H3] Current Condition | - Additional Mechanic Order menggunakan LOV Activity Type dari master Maintenance Category. | - Additional Inspection menggunakan LOV Activity Type dari master yang sama. | - Tidak ada mekanisme untuk membedakan Activity Type yang diperuntukkan untuk Additional Mechanic Order dan Additional Inspection. | - User dapat melihat pilihan Activity Type yang sebenarnya tidak relevan dengan proses bisnis pada fitur yang digunakan. | [H3] Expected Condition | - System menyediakan mekanisme untuk membedakan Activity Type yang dapat digunakan pada: | - Additional Mechanic Order | - Additional Inspection | - Saat user membuka LOV Activity Type pada Additional Mechanic Order, hanya Activity Type yang sesuai untuk Mechanic Order yang ditampilkan. | - Saat user membuka LOV Activity Type pada Additional Inspection, hanya Activity Type yang sesuai untuk Inspection yang ditampilkan. | - Mekanisme pemisahan data akan ditentukan pada tahap desain teknis (misalnya menggunakan flag, mapping, master data terpisah, atau pendekatan lainnya). | [H3] Business Benefit | - Mengurangi kemungkinan user memilih Activity Type yang tidak sesuai. | - Meningkatkan kualitas data yang terbentuk dari Additional Mechanic Order dan Additional Inspection. | - Mendukung pemisahan proses bisnis antara aktivitas inspection dan order. | - Mempermudah pengelolaan master data di masa mendatang. | [H3] Notes | Saat ini sumber data Activity Type berasal dari master  | Maintenance Category |  pada Maintenance Strategy dan digunakan oleh lebih dari satu fitur. Diperlukan kajian desain untuk menentukan pendekatan terbaik dalam memisahkan daftar nilai yang digunakan oleh masing-masing proses bisnis.

summary: [MOBILE] Form Package — Tap Form Card: Render Form from Cosmos Snapshot
desc: [H2] Background | After a mechanic assigns themselves to a form via "Assign to Me", they should be able to tap the form card to open and fill the form. The form content is rendered from the Cosmos snapshot created at plan-save time, identified by  | FormSubmissionId | . The existing Form Builder renderer component is reused for this. | [H2] Objective | Implement form card tap navigation that opens the form renderer using the  | FormSubmissionId |  from the form card, rendering the form from the Cosmos snapshot stored in  | maintenance-execution | . | [H2] Scope | - Product: Digiman+ Mobile | - Screen: PM Shutdown Work Detail → Tab Form → Form card tap → Form renderer | - Renderer: Reuse existing Form Builder renderer component | - Data source:  | maintenance-execution |  Cosmos, keyed by  | FormSubmissionId | - Depends on:  |   (Tab Form UI),  |   (Mulai button — TaskPersonalizedLog session) | [H2] Requirements | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Tap guard || Card tap only navigates to form if current user has  | TaskPersonalized |  for this  | TaskId | . If not assigned, tap does nothing (no navigation, no error). | 2 || Navigation || Tap card → navigate to form renderer screen, passing  | FormSubmissionId |  as the key | 3 || Renderer reuse || Form is rendered using existing Form Builder renderer component. No new renderer to be built. | 4 || Snapshot source || Form template fetched from  | maintenance-execution |  Cosmos using  | FormSubmissionId |  — not from  | maintenance-strategy | . | 5 || Offline support || If form template already in local cache (from offline sync), render from cache. No network call required. | 6 || Auto-save || Existing auto-save behavior applies — no additional implementation needed. | 7 || Submitted form || If  | Task.Status = Complete | , form is rendered in read-only / view mode. | 8 || Start button || After form renderer opens, mechanic sees a "Start" button to start the session. Session logic (TaskPersonalizedLog creation + auto-close) is handled by  |  . | [H2] Business Rules | - FormSubmissionId |  is the key for all Cosmos snapshot operations — mobile must not construct its own snapshot path. | - Current user must have an active  | TaskPersonalized |  record for the task before tap is allowed. | - Multi-mechanic visibility: mechanics can see fields filled by other mechanics (existing Form Builder behavior — no additional implementation needed). | - Submitted form is view-only — no editing or re-submission possible (reopen deferred to next MVP). | - Session tracking (TaskPersonalizedLog) starts when mechanic taps Start Icon inside the form, not when the card is tapped. | [H2] Figma | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Tapping an unassigned card produces no navigation. | - Tapping an assigned card navigates to form renderer with correct  | FormSubmissionId | . | - Form content matches Cosmos snapshot from  | maintenance-execution |  — not  | maintenance-strategy | . | - Form renders correctly offline using cached snapshot. | - Submitted form opens in read-only mode. | - "Start" button visible in form renderer for non-submitted forms. | - No regression on existing Form Builder renderer behavior. | - Auto-save behavior works as expected (existing). | [H2] Prerequisite untuk Mengisi Form: Assign to Me DAN Tap Start | Form card SELALU bisa dibuka (tap untuk view) oleh semua user yang punya akses PM Shutdown workcard. Namun untuk bisa mengisi (edit) form, ada dua prerequisite yang harus dipenuhi: | Kondisi || Yang Bisa Dilakukan | Mechanic belum Assign to Me || Buka form untuk VIEW saja. Form read-only. Tombol Start tidak muncul. | Sudah Assign to Me, belum tap Start || Buka form untuk VIEW. Tombol Start muncul. Form masih read-only sampai Start di-tap. | Sudah Assign to Me DAN sudah tap Start || Buka form dan ISI form. Tombol Start muncul setiap kali membuka form. | Form sudah di-Submit (Complete) || Buka form untuk VIEW saja. Form read-only permanen. Tombol Start tidak muncul. | [H3] Behavior tap form card per kondisi | - Belum Assign to Me: tap form card -> buka form dalam read-only mode. Tombol Start tidak ada. Mechanic bisa melihat template form tapi tidak bisa mengetik. | - Sudah Assign to Me, belum Start: tap form card -> buka form dalam read-only mode. Tombol Start muncul di bawah. Tap Start -> form menjadi editable. | - Sudah Start: tap form card -> buka form langsung editable. Tombol Start tetap muncul (untuk tracking sesi saat dibuka kembali). | - Form Complete: tap form card -> buka form dalam read-only mode permanent. Badge "Complete" pada form card. | [H3] Mengapa dua langkah (Assign + Start)? | - Assign to Me: mencatat mechanic sebagai pengerjaan form (membuat TaskPersonalized). Sistem tahu siapa yang bertanggung jawab. | - Tap Start: sesi kerja dimulai dan dicatat (membuat TaskPersonalizedLog). Digunakan untuk activity tracking dan laporan aktivitas. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria (tambahan) | - User yang belum Assign to Me: form terbuka read-only, tidak ada tombol Start, tidak bisa mengetik. | - User yang sudah Assign, belum Start: form terbuka read-only, tombol Start muncul, form jadi editable setelah Start. | - Form Complete: selalu read-only untuk semua user, tombol Start tidak ada. | - Widget test: read-only state, Start button visibility, editable state setelah Start.

subtasks.summary: MOBILE: Submit Form UI — tap Submit, Summary review, confirm dialog, offline queue
subtasks.desc: [H2] Background | Setelah mechanic selesai mengisi form, salah satu mechanic (perwakilan) melakukan Submit. Submit mengunci form secara final. Sebelum submit, mechanic bisa review via Summary. Seluruh flow ini harus bekerja offline. | [H2] UI Flow | - [1] Mechanic tap tombol "Submit" di form detail screen (hanya muncul jika mechanic sudah assign dan sudah tap Start). | - [2] Sistem cek client-side: semua mandatory fields terisi di local device? Jika belum: tampilkan daftar fields yang belum terisi, tombol Submit disabled. | - [3] Sebelum submit final: tampilkan Summary screen (reuse existing Form Submission summary component) untuk review seluruh isian form. | - [4] Mechanic tap "Confirm Submit" di Summary screen. | - [5] Request Submit dikirim ke server (POST /api/form-submissions/{id}/submit). Jika offline: di-queue, dikirim saat koneksi tersedia. | - [6] Setelah submit berhasil: form card di Tab Form menampilkan status "Complete", form terbuka dalam read-only mode. | [H2] State management | Kondisi || Tombol Submit | Mechanic belum assign atau belum tap Start || Tidak muncul | Ada mandatory field yang belum terisi (per local data) || Muncul tapi disabled, dengan hint | Semua mandatory field terisi || Enabled | Form sudah di-Submit (Complete) || Tidak muncul, form read-only | [H2] Duplicate submit handling di mobile | Jika dua mechanic sama-sama tap Submit saat offline: keduanya meng-queue request. Yang pertama tiba di server yang berlaku. Yang kedua akan mendapat response 200 (idempotent) — tidak ada error ditampilkan ke user. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Tombol Submit muncul hanya untuk mechanic yang sudah assign dan tap Start. | - Tombol Submit disabled jika ada mandatory field kosong (berdasarkan local data), dengan hint fields yang kurang. | - Summary screen tampil sebelum confirm submit — reuse existing component. | - Submit bekerja offline: di-queue dan dikirim saat koneksi tersedia. | - Setelah submit: form card status = Complete, form read-only. | - Widget test: disabled state, enabled state, offline queue behavior.

From  | forms[].assignees | . | 6 || 3-dot menu (···) || Right side of card, same row as assignee avatars | Status badge mapping: | Condition || Badge | No assignees, Task.Status = Open || Not Assigned (grey) | Assignee exists OR Task.Status = In Progress || No badge — meta info shows "Opened by [name]" | Task.Status = Complete || Submitted (green) | 3-dot menu options: | Condition || "Assign to Me" || "Assign to.." | Current user not assigned || Active || Active | Current user already assigned || Disabled (dimmed) || Active | Form status = Submitted || Disabled || Disabled | Finish Execution button: | Condition || State || Hint | Mandatory form incomplete || Disabled || "⚠ X mandatory form belum submitted" | All conditions met || Enabled || — | [H2] Business Rules | - Tab Form data loaded from  | forms |  array in workcard detail response. | - Empty state shown if  | forms |  array is empty (plan has no bundle). | - Offline: use local cache from last sync. desc: [H2] Background | When the Planner enters Step 2 (Choose Form) of the Create Daily Planning modal stepper, the system must fetch the relevant form list from BE. subtasks.summary: Cache strategy: populated on first 'Choose Form' click, hit on re-open, invalidate on Equipment change
subtasks.desc: Scope: | Frontend.Web.DigitalPlanning |  — TanStack Query cache behavior for the form list. | Cache lifecycle: | - First click: |  "Choose Form" clicked with  | equipmentModel |  X → GET fires, result cached under key  | ['form-list', X, serviceType] | . | - Re-open same modal (same equipment): |  Modal closed and re-opened without changing Equipment →  | enabled |  becomes true again, TanStack Query finds the cache entry → no network request, data displayed immediately. | - Equipment changed: |  Planner changes the Equipment field in Create Plan form → call  | queryClient.invalidateQueries(['form-list']) |  → cache cleared. Next "Choose Form" click fires a fresh GET. | - Stale time: |  Set  | staleTime: Infinity |  so the cache never auto-refetches during the same modal session (data should reflect the form catalog at the moment the modal was first opened). | - Selection preserved: |  Per epic rule — "Filter tidak mereset selection" — form checkbox state is stored separately (Pinia store or local ref), never cleared by cache invalidation.

summary: [FE] Form Bundle — Choose Form: Step 2 Stepper in Create Plan Modal with Mandatory Toggle
desc: [H2] Background | As part of the Form Bundle Integration for PM Shutdown, Planners need to select multiple forms to attach to a plan during plan creation. Asset Type column and Filter panel have been removed from the design. | [H2] Objective | Implement Step 2 of the Create Daily Planning modal stepper, displaying a form selection table with mandatory toggle per row, allowing the Planner to build a form bundle before saving the plan. | [H2] Scope | - Product: Digiplan Web | - Screen: Create Daily Planning modal → Step 2 (Choose Form) | - Design reference: Figma — Daily Planning, node  | 2128:11941 |  ( | Digiman+ - Daily Planning – Figma | ) | [H2] Requirements | No || Requirement || Detail | 1 || Stepper || Create Daily Planning modal uses a stepper. Step 1 = plan info (existing fields). Step 2 = Choose Form. | 2 || Form table columns || Checkbox, Form Name, Asset Model, Service Type, Maintenance Category Type, Mandatory | 3 || Asset Type column || Removed from design |  — do NOT include | 4 || Filter panel || Removed from design |  — do NOT include | 5 || Mandatory column || Toggle (switcher) per row — default OFF for all forms | 6 || Multi-select || Planner can check any number of forms via checkbox. Bottom right: Cancel + Save/Continue button. | 8 || Empty state || If API returns no forms: render BTech No Content empty state with label and description | 9 || Search || Search input field available inside Step 2 to filter form list by name | [H2] Business Rules | - All mandatory toggles default to OFF — no pre-selection or auto-default. | - Planner can proceed with 0 forms selected — valid state. | - Once Planner confirms and plan is saved, form bundle cannot be modified. | - Filter does not affect selection state of already-checked forms (filter removed, but search does not reset selection). | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Step 2 of stepper renders form table with correct columns (no Asset Type, no Filter panel). | - Mandatory toggle defaults to OFF for all rows. | - Multi-select works — footer count updates correctly as rows are checked/unchecked. | - Footer shows "# form selected | # mandatory" correctly. | - Empty state renders when API returns no forms. | - Search filters visible rows without resetting selection state. | - Back button returns to Step 1 without losing Step 1 data. | - No regression on existing Create Daily Planning Step 1 fields.

summary: [BE] Form Package — Extend Finish Execution: Add Mandatory Form Gate
desc:
[H2] Background | Currently,  | POST /api/executionplanning/finish/:userId |  validates that all backlog tasks are completed before allowing Finish Execution. With the form package integration, an additional gate is required: all mandatory forms in the package must have  | Task.Status = Complete |  before Finish Execution is permitted. | [H2] Objective | Add a mandatory form completion check to the Finish Execution endpoint. Finish Execution must be blocked if any  | Task |  linked to a  | FormSubmission |  with  | IsMandatory = true |  has a status other than  | Complete | . | [H2] Scope | - Service:  | dplan | - Modified endpoint:  | POST /api/executionplanning/finish/:userId?ver=v1 | - Data source:  | maintenance-execution |  SQL | [H2] Requirements | No || Validation || Existing / New || Behavior if failed | 1 || All backlog tasks completed || Existing || Return 400 with existing error message | 2 || All mandatory forms  | Task.Status = Complete || New || Return 400 with error message and list of incomplete mandatory forms | Error response when mandatory forms are incomplete: | No || Field || Type || Description | 1 || message || String || "Finish Execution cannot be performed. One or more mandatory forms are not yet submitted." | 2 || incompleteForms || Array || List of  | { taskId, formName } |  for mandatory forms not yet Complete | Query logic for new validation: | SELECT t.Id as TaskId, fs.FormName
FROM Task t
JOIN FormSubmission fs ON fs.TaskId = t.Id
JOIN WorkOrder wo ON wo.Id = t.WorkOrderId
WHERE wo.PlanId = :planId
  AND fs.IsMandatory = 1
  AND t.Status != 'Complete' | [H2] Business Rules | - If a plan has no form package, the mandatory form gate is skipped — Finish Execution proceeds with existing validation only. | - Both validations (backlog + mandatory form) are checked. If both fail, return single 400 with both error details. | - Offline: client-side check mirrors this logic using local data. Server performs final validation on sync. | - TenantCode isolation from JWT. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria | - Finish Execution blocked when any mandatory form  | Task.Status != Complete | . | - Error response includes  | incompleteForms |  array with  | taskId |  and  | formName | . | - Finish Execution succeeds when all mandatory forms are  | Complete |  and all backlogs done. | - Plans with no form package: Finish Execution unaffected by new gate. | - Optional forms ( | IsMandatory = false | ) do not block Finish Execution. | - No regression on existing Finish Execution flow. | [H2] Finish Execution — Offline Behavior | Finish Execution bisa dilakukan offline. Validasi dilakukan di sisi device (client-side) menggunakan data lokal: | - Cek lokal: backlog execution selesai? + semua mandatory form Task.Status = Complete? | - Jika kondisi terpenuhi: aksi Finish Execution dibolehkan offline. | - Aksi di-queue dan dikirim ke server saat koneksi tersedia. | - Server melakukan final validation saat sync. Jika tidak sesuai: server reject + notify mobile. | Prinsip: offline-first berlaku penuh. Client-side check adalah gate — user tidak perlu online selama data lokal memenuhi syarat. | [H2] packageSyncStatus = pending memblokir Finish Execution | Jika packageSyncStatus = "pending" (ada PlanForm tapi Task belum terbuat): Finish Execution di-block dengan pesan: "Form package is not yet available. Please try again later or contact your admin." | [H2] Access Control | Siapa || Finish Execution | Supervisor / Foreman || Selalu bisa | Mechanic || Bisa jika memiliki permission PM_Shutdown_Finish_Execution yang dikonfigurasi di level akses user | Permission Finish Execution untuk Mechanic dikonfigurasi di level akses user — bukan per workcard atau per site. Mengikuti sistem permission management yang sudah ada di Digiman+. | [H2] DueDate WorkOrder | DueDate WorkOrder bersifat informasi saja — tidak ada enforcement atau blocking otomatis jika DueDate terlewati. Mechanic dan Supervisor tetap bisa melanjutkan dan menyelesaikan eksekusi setelah DueDate. | [H2] Acceptance Criteria (tambahan) | - Finish Execution dapat dilakukan offline jika validasi client-side terpenuhi, dan di-queue ke server. | - packageSyncStatus = pending: Finish Execution di-block di server dengan error message yang sesuai. | - Mechanic tanpa permission PM_Shutdown_Finish_Execution mendapat 403 dari server. | - DueDate lewat: tidak memblokir Finish Execution — informasi saja. | - Test: offline finish (success), packageSyncStatus pending (blocked), mechanic without permission (403), DueDate overdue (no block).
